page: Microsoft Azure E160ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-series virtual machine configured with 160 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 AMD processor architecture. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 1280.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio.

The server includes 9449 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, supporting high-throughput disk configurations. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard graphics processing units allocated to this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E160ads_v7 offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with substantial local storage on a single virtual machine. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, enterprise applications, and data warehousing operations that require significant RAM and local disk space without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E160ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, powered by AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs and 1280.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. For storage-heavy operations, the instance is equipped with 9449 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale enterprise applications, in-memory databases, and extensive data processing tasks. The server is optimized for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and local storage performance without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E160ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 1280 GB of RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage for enterprise database workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring one hundred and sixty dedicated cores and substantial local storage for enterprise database workloads.
